test.command: bash tests/phase-guard.test.sh && bash tests/mission-continue.test.sh && bash tests/mission-reminder.test.sh && .factory/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v
test.details: phase-guard: 207/207 PASS, mission-continue: 143/143 PASS, mission-reminder: 120/120 PASS, test_engine: 166/166 PASS — total 636 tests

nonBlockingIssues.description: CI 'Install pytest' step missing 'shell: bash' — inconsistent with other steps but functionally correct on all 3 OS targets.

README.md defense table shows 10 defenses while CLAUDE.md says 'Eight hook-enforced defense layers'. The extra 2 (Symlink Protection, Phase Transitions) are real defenses but aren't numbered in CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md. Minor documentation alignment opportunity.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Remove stale migration note that says old bash hooks (.sh) should be preserved as reference. The .sh files no longer exist in the repo — hooks.json exclusively uses .py hooks now.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md v0.5.0 → v1.0 Migration Notes says 'Old bash hooks (phase-guard.sh, mission-continue.sh, mission-reminder.sh) should be preserved as reference but hooks.json must point to new Python hooks'. However, no .sh hook files remain in hooks/ directory.

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml uses '.factory/.venv/bin/python -m pytest' while CI uses 'python3 -m pytest' — different invocation paths.
rejectedObservations.reason: By design. Local dev uses project-local venv (ensuring pytest is installed), CI uses actions/setup-python which provides python3 in PATH. Both approaches are correct for their respective environments.
